nodes.label: Release Reconciliation
nodes.rationale: Public releases are checksummed and activated only after scores, dates, opponents, period totals, team totals, player totals, play-by-play, and reviewed reports reconcile.

nodes.label: High-Stakes Decision Disclaimer
nodes.rationale: The archive is informational and entertainment-oriented and should not be used for betting, financial, legal, medical, or other high-stakes decisions.

nodes.label: Unsupported Claim Refusal
nodes.rationale: Unsupported tactical or causal claims are declined and source/provider terms remain controlling.

nodes.label: Deterministic Answer Mode
nodes.rationale: Deterministic mode bypasses planning and synthesis while preserving factual answers.

nodes.label: Immutable Release Boundary
nodes.rationale: Offline normalization, reconciliation, review, hash verification, and transactional idempotent loading isolate public traffic from mutable ingestion.

nodes.label: Runtime Request Flow
nodes.rationale: Optional retrieval, model, and cache dependencies must not prevent deterministic factual answers.

nodes.label: Immutable Qdrant RAG Indexing
nodes.rationale: Stable aliases are promoted only after collection counts validate; Postgres remains authoritative and can rebuild the indexes.

nodes.label: Deterministic Fallback
nodes.rationale: Vector, model, Redis-budget, timeout, parsing, and validation failures return deterministic output with degraded=true.

nodes.label: Answer and Retrieval Release Gates
nodes.rationale: Correctness, citation, recall, refusal, latency, and error thresholds gate promotion.

nodes.label: Read-Only MCP Tools
nodes.rationale: Tools do not mutate game data, execute shell commands, or write to the filesystem.

nodes.label: Failed Deployment Rollback
nodes.rationale: Keep the previous image and validated release available because migrations are expand-only and aliases can be switched back.

nodes.label: Database Restore and Qdrant Rebuild
nodes.rationale: The immutable hash-bound bundle is the recovery source of truth and the loader is transactional and idempotent.

nodes.label: Dependency Outage Fallbacks
nodes.rationale: Optional Qdrant, Redis, OpenRouter, and Sentry failures preserve availability or deterministic facts.

nodes.label: Transactional Release Load
nodes.rationale: All release rows appear together or none appear, preventing a half-archive from becoming public.
